title: Close Curly Brace and the Following Keywords Should Be on the Same Line
description: This rule enforces the coding convention of placing the closing curly brace on the same line as the following 'else', 'catch', or 'finally' keywords. This promotes efficient collaboration within the team by ensuring a consistent coding style.
recommendation: To fix violations of this rule, ensure that the closing curly brace and the following 'else', 'catch', or 'finally' keywords are located on the same line.
impact: Non-compliance with this rule may make the code more difficult to read and understand, potentially slowing down the team's collaboration and productivity.

checks.message: else should be on the same line as closing brace
